---
config:
  flowchart:
    htmlLabels: false
---

# Wrapping Up

## Knowing and Deciding in Uncertainty 

Uncertainty is the entrepreneur’s native terrain.  
You’ve seen that it’s not the same as risk, and that pretending otherwise only leads to trouble.  

In this part, you built tools for working with that uncertainty:  

- **Uncertainty vs. Risk.** You learned to name the difference, so you don’t confuse probabilities with unknowns.  
- **Knowledge and Epistemologies.** You explored the ways we come to know — from authority to observation to imagination — and how each can serve or mislead you.  
- **Judgment.** You practiced weighing what you know, what you believe, and when to act.  
- **Belief Revision.** You learned to update within a frame (Bayesian updating) and revise the frame itself when reality no longer fits.  

<br>
![](images/wrap-knowing-revising.svg){width=70%}

The point isn’t to eliminate uncertainty — you can’t.  
The point is to **navigate it with clarity and confidence**, making calls with eyes open instead of guessing in the fog.  

```{mermaid}

flowchart TD
   %% === Core structure ===
    A["`**Knowledge**
        *(Uncertainty)*`"] --> B{"`**Judgment**
                                         *(explore or*
                                         *hypothesize)*
                                    `"}
    
    %% --- Judgment branches ---
    B --> experimentLoop["`Low Knowledge
                      High Uncertainty`"]
    B --> lbdLoop["`High Knowledge
                      Low Uncertainty`"]
    
    %% --- Feedback paths ---
    experimentLoop --> E["Experimental Outcome"]
    lbdLoop --> F["Market Outcome"]
    
    %% --- Cognitive interpretation ---
    E --> G{"Cognitive Frame"}
    F --> G
    
    %% --- Frame test ---
    G --> H["Breaks"]
    G --> I["Holds"]
    
    %% --- Belief adjustment paths ---
    H --> J["Belief Revision"]
    I --> K["Bayesian Updating"]
    
    %% --- Loop closure ---
    J --> G
    K --> A

    %% === Visual styling ===
    style A fill:#007da3,stroke:#000000,stroke-width:1px,color:#ffffff
    style B fill:#FFFBE6,stroke:#ED8936,stroke-width:1px
    style experimentLoop fill:#E6F7FA,stroke:#007BA7,stroke-width:1px
    style lbdLoop fill:#E6F7FA,stroke:#007BA7,stroke-width:1px
    style E fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style F fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style G fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style H fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style I fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style J fill:#caf0f4,stroke:#000000,stroke-width:0.5px,color:#000000
    style K fill:#caf0f4,stroke:#000000,stroke-width:0.5px,color:#000000
```

> **Figure X. The Epistemic Cycle of Entrepreneurship**  
> Entrepreneurs begin in uncertainty — a state defined by what is not yet known.  
> Through judgment, they decide whether their current knowledge is sufficient to act (*learning by doing*) or still too thin, requiring further *learning by experimenting*.  
> Each form of action generates outcomes that are interpreted through a cognitive frame.  
> If the frame holds, Bayesian updating refines knowledge; if it breaks, belief revision rebuilds it.  
> Both paths loop back to renew knowledge and reduce uncertainty, sustaining the ongoing cycle of entrepreneurial knowing and acting.



```{mermaid}
#| label: fig-relational-model
#| fig-cap: "Relational model of knowing, judging, acting, and updating under uncertainty."

flowchart TD
   %% === Core structure ===
    A["Knowledge / Uncertainty"] --> B{"`Judgment
                                         (explore or
                                         hypothesize)
                                    `"}
    B --> experimentLoop["`Low Knowledge
                      High Uncertainty`"]
    B --> lbdLoop["`High Knowledge
                      Low Uncertainty`"]
    experimentLoop --> E["Experimental Outcome"]
    lbdLoop --> F["Market Outcome"]
    E --> G{"Cognitive Frame"}
    F --> G
    G --> H["Breaks"]
    G --> I["Holds"]
    H --> J["Belief Revision"]
    I --> K["Bayesian Updating"]
    J --> A
    K --> A
    style A fill:#007da3,stroke:#000000,stroke-width:1px,color:#ffffff
    style B fill:#FFFBE6,stroke:#ED8936,stroke-width:1px
    style experimentLoop fill:#E6F7FA,stroke:#007BA7,stroke-width:1px
    style lbdLoop fill:#E6F7FA,stroke:#007BA7,stroke-width:1px
    style E fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style F fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style G fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style H fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style I fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style J fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style K fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
```


```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    %% --- Core flow ---
    A["Uncertainty"] --> B["Knowledge"]
    B --> C{"Judgment: Do we know enough to act?"}

    %% --- Judgment branch ---
    C -->|Low knowledge / high uncertainty| L["Learning Loop (exploratory experiments)"]
    C -->|High knowledge / low uncertainty| D["Doing Loop (entrepreneurial action)"]

    %% --- Learning loop feedback ---
    L --> E["Evidence"]
    E --> F{"Frame holds?"}
    F -->|Yes| G["Bayesian Updating"]
    F -->|No| H["Belief Revision"]
    G --> I["Refined Knowledge"]
    H --> I
    I --> C

    %% --- Doing loop feedback ---
    D --> J["Market Outcomes"]
    J --> K["New Uncertainty"]
    K --> A

    %% --- Loop labels (visual anchors) ---
    L -.-> Llabel["Learning Loop"]
    D -.-> Dlabel["Doing Loop"]

    %% --- Styling (safe) ---
    style A fill:#E2E8F0,stroke:#1A202C,stroke-width:1px
    style B fill:#E2E8F0,stroke:#1A202C,stroke-width:1px
    style C fill:#FFFBE6,stroke:#ED8936,stroke-width:1px
    style L fill:#E6F7FA,stroke:#007BA7,stroke-width:1px
    style D fill:#E6F7FA,stroke:#007BA7,stroke-width:1px
    style E,F,G,H,I,J,K fill:#F9F9F9,stroke:#CBD5E0,stroke-width:0.5px
    style Llabel fill:none,stroke:none,color:#007BA7
    style Dlabel fill:none,stroke:none,color:#007BA7
```    


## Looking Ahead  

Part II shifts from *how to know* toward *how to ask*.  
Once you accept uncertainty and learn to manage your beliefs, the next move is to frame the **right questions**:  

- What don’t I know yet?  
- Which unknowns matter most right now?  
- How do I turn those into testable hypotheses?  

That’s where we go next. Because asking the right questions is what turns uncertainty into insight.  

---
